story.iWant: to maintain theme version history with timestamps and preserve all versions
story.soThat: users can track changes over time and have a complete audit trail for rollback capabilities

tasks.task: Implement Version Manager - Directory Setup: Check/create .slide-builder/theme-history/, log creation to status.yaml
Implement Version Manager - Version Number Resolution: Read meta.version, parse, increment, handle edge cases
Implement Version Manager - Save Version: Generate filename theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json, copy theme content, write file, verify
Implement Version Manager - Meta Version Update: Update theme.json meta.version and lastModified after version save
Implement Version Manager - List Versions: Scan theme-history/, parse filenames, sort by version, return list
Integrate Version Manager with /setup Workflow: Save v1 to theme-history after theme creation
Integrate Version Manager with /theme-edit Workflow: Verify version save before changes, increment version after approval
Implement Status Logger for Version Operations: Log version save actions to status.yaml history
Testing - Version History Verification: Full test coverage of all ACs

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a theme change is made (via setup or edit), when the theme is saved, then a timestamped copy is saved to theme-history/
Version files follow naming convention: theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json
Version number increments automatically based on theme meta.version
The theme.json meta.version is updated after each change
All previous versions are preserved in theme-history/ (never deleted)
The theme-history/ directory maintains all versions with dates visible in filenames

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification
doc.section: Story 6.3: Theme Version History, Services and Modules, Data Models and Contracts
doc.snippet: Version Manager module specification with save/list/restore operations. Version file naming convention theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json. All versions preserved, never deleted.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.section: Project Structure, Version Control Strategy (ADR-006), State File Patterns
doc.snippet: Theme stored at .slide-builder/theme.json, version history in theme-history/ (gitignored per ADR-006). Status.yaml tracks all significant actions with ISO 8601 timestamps.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: User story for maintaining timestamped version history. Filename pattern theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json. History directory maintains all versions.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR10: System maintains theme version history. NFR13: Theme files remain local. NFR15: Theme changes don't corrupt existing slides.

doc.title: Story 6.2: Theme Editing
doc.snippet: Story 6.2 defined Version Manager interface that 6.3 implements. Save operation: input ThemeJson, output versionFile path and newVersionNumber. Workflow calls version save in Phase 1.

doc.title: Story 6.1: Theme Summary View
doc.snippet: Established Theme Loader module and status.yaml update patterns. ANSI color swatch generation with 24-bit true color. Status history append format documented.

file.reason: Contains meta.version field that drives version numbering. Must read current version before save, update after save.
Theme-edit workflow Phase 1 calls Version Manager to save current theme before modifications. This story implements the actual save logic.
Setup workflow Step 6.3 saves first theme version to history. This story ensures the directory and file operations are correct.
Version operations must log to status.yaml history. Pattern: { action: "Theme v{N} saved to history", timestamp: "ISO8601" }
Reuse Theme Loader pattern for reading theme.json to extract version before save operation.

node.purpose: HTML-to-image conversion (not used in this story)
Google Slides API (not used in this story)

constraints.constraint: Version files MUST follow naming: theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json
Version saved BEFORE changes applied in /theme-edit - always have rollback
All previous versions preserved in theme-history/ - never deleted
theme.json meta.version MUST be incremented on each change
theme-history/ is gitignored per ADR-006 (user content, not framework)
status.yaml tracks all significant actions with ISO 8601 timestamps
Theme files remain local to user's machine (NFR13)
Theme changes don't corrupt existing slides (NFR15)
Performance: Version save < 100ms, Version list < 100ms (local file operations)

interface.name: Version Manager - Save
interface.signature: saveVersion(theme: ThemeJson): { versionFile: string, newVersionNumber: string }

interface.name: Version Manager - List
interface.signature: listVersions(): Array<{ version: string, date: string, path: string }>

interface.name: Theme JSON Schema
interface.signature: { meta: { name, version, created, lastModified, sources, changeNotes }, colors, typography, shapes, layouts }

interface.name: Version File Naming
interface.signature: theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json where N is version number, date is creation date

tests.standards: Manual testing per Epic 6 Tech Spec Test Strategy. Each acceptance criterion verified through file system inspection and workflow execution. Test scenarios include: version creation on setup, version creation on edit, version preservation across multiple edits, correct filename format, meta.version increment verification.

locations.location: Manual verification via file system inspection

ideas.idea: Run /setup, verify theme-v1-{date}.json created in theme-history/
Check filename matches pattern theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json exactly
Run /theme-edit twice, verify versions increment v1→v2→v3
After edit approval, verify theme.json meta.version updated
Run multiple edits, verify all version files preserved (v1, v2, v3 all exist)
List theme-history/ directory, verify dates visible in all filenames
Delete theme-history/, run setup, verify directory created and v1 saved
Manually set theme.json version to 999, run edit, verify v1000 created correctly
Verify cancel in /theme-edit does NOT create spurious version
